title: Sam Altman throws shade at Anthropic’s cyber model, Mythos: ‘fear-based marketing’
description: The AI industry's mudslinging continues.

title: Mozilla Used Anthropic’s Mythos to Find and Fix 151 Bugs in Firefox
description: The Firefox team doesn’t think emerging AI capabilities will upend cybersecurity long term, but they warn that software developers are likely in for a rocky transition.

title: Microsoft removes Call of Duty from Game Pass, lowers subscription pricing
description: New Xbox CEO says subscription "has become too expensive for too many players."

title: Framework Laptop 13 Pro is a major overhaul for the modular, upgradeable laptop
description: Laptop includes Core Ultra Series 3 CPUs, a bigger battery, and a touchscreen.

title: Framework Laptop 16 upgrades make it look less like an unfinished prototype
description: A lower-end Ryzen AI 340 CPU option will also bring the price down, for now.

title: Internal emails show how Amazon raises prices across the Internet, lawsuit says
description: “It’s working!“: Amazon emails with vendors show pattern of disappearing deals.

title: Tim Cook’s Legacy Is Turning Apple Into a Subscription
description: The soon-to-exit Apple CEO went all in on services. Now, the incoming CEO, John Ternus, will need to embrace the AI era.

title: Building agent-first governance and security
description: As AI agents increasingly work alongside humans across organizations, companies could be inadvertently opening a new attack surface. title: Clarifai deletes 3 million photos that OkCupid provided to train facial recognition AI, report says
description: The photo deletion comes after an FTC settlement with Clarifai. The company had asked OkCupid — whose executives had invested in Clarifai — to share data in 2014, according to court documents.

title: Anthropic gets $5B investment from Amazon, will use it to buy Amazon chips
description: Anthropic secures 5 gigawatts of Amazon’s custom silicon as Claude demand soars.

title: AI Dungeon maker Latitude unveils Voyage, a platform for creating AI-powered RPGs
description: Latitude's new AI-native platform, Voyage, aims to help gamers create their very own role-playing game.

title: Bond, a new social media platform, wants to use AI to help you kick your doomscrolling habit
description: Bond wants you to get off the couch and get back into the real world, its creator says. The new platform's AI system is designed to motivate users to do things away from the app.

title: YouTube expands its AI likeness detection technology to celebrities
description: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection tool to celebrities, giving talent and their reps a way to find and remove deepfakes.

title: The Download: turning down human noise, and LA’s stunning subway upgrade
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The noise we make is hurting animals. title: This Scammer Used an AI-Generated MAGA Girl to Grift ‘Super Dumb’ Men
description: A med student says he’s made thousands of dollars selling photos and videos of a young conservative woman he created using generative tools. He’s not alone.

title: Digging for clues about the North Pole’s past
description: In the past, even with an icebreaker and during peak melt season, getting to the North Pole wasn’t a sure bet. title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
description: An autonomous robot from the company Honor ran a half marathon in 50:26, beating the human record by 7 minutes.

title: Prego Has a Dinner-Conversation-Recording Device, Capisce?
description: The pasta-sauce company has partnered with the nonprofit StoryCorps on a device designed to record family conversations around the table and save them for all time.

title: The Download: murderous ‘mirror’ bacteria, and Chinese workers fighting AI doubles
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. title: Colossal Biosciences said it cloned red wolves. Is it for real?
description: If you want to capture something wolflike, it’s best to embark before dawn. title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
